title: MZpack Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of MZpack pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
MZpack Pricing Overview

MZpack is a NinjaTrader 8 order flow indicator and automated strategy platform built by a Georgia-based team since 2016. It offers one of the most accessible entry points to professional-grade order flow analysis, with a $49/month starting price for its indicators module and a $399 lifetime license option that undercuts comparable desktop platforms like Bookmap and ATAS. The platform includes six purpose-built modules (mzFootprint, mzVolumeProfile, mzBigTrade, mzMarketDepth, mzVolumeDelta, mzDeltaDivergence), a public API for custom development, and optional automated strategies—but only runs on NinjaTrader 8.

All MZpack Plans Compared

MZpack offers four primary pricing tiers, each designed for different trader types and commitment levels:

Plan Price What You Get Best For
Free Trial $0 14-day full access to all indicators; no credit card required; permanently free limited version after trial First-time evaluators; traders testing before commit
Indicators Monthly $49/mo All six indicator modules; backtesting; alerts; API access; updates included Active discretionary traders wanting month-to-month flexibility
Indicators Lifetime €399 (~$435 USD) Identical to monthly tier but perpetual license; one-time payment; future updates included Long-term traders planning to use MZpack for years; breaks even vs. monthly in ~9 months
Strategies Lifetime €399 (~$435 USD) MZpack Strategies automated trading module with Divergence algo; no indicators included Traders wanting systematic, hands-off order flow execution without discretionary indicators
Bundle Lifetime €599 (~$655 USD) Indicators + Strategies combined; both modules; perpetual license; everything MZpack offers Hybrid traders blending discretionary analysis with automated order flow setups

Monthly to Annual Conversion: At $49/month, annual commitment would be ~$588/year—making the €399 (~$435) lifetime license a 26% saving if you plan to trade for at least a year. If you trade actively for 3+ years, the lifetime tier saves $1,200+ vs. staying on monthly.

Free Plan / Free Trial

MZpack's free offering is unusually generous for order flow software:

  • 14-day free trial: Full access to all six indicator modules. No credit card required to start.
  • Permanently free limited version: After the trial expires, you retain access to a stripped-down free tier with basic indicators—enough to paper trade and learn the UI without paying.
  • No payment lock-in: Trial doesn't auto-convert to a paid subscription; you must manually upgrade.

This is a major advantage for discretionary traders evaluating whether order flow analysis is worth learning. You get two weeks to backtest, paper trade, and decide without financial commitment. The permanently free tier means you can stay on indefinitely if you're not yet ready to pay but want to keep a low-cost license active for education or leisure trading.

For beginners: The free trial is essential. Order flow indicators (footprint, DOM imbalance, cumulative delta) have a steep learning curve, and MZpack assumes you already understand these concepts. The 14 days gives you time to assess whether the learning investment makes sense before paying $49/month or €399 upfront.

Hidden Costs and Fees

MZpack pricing is relatively transparent, but several real costs exist outside the headline tier price:

  • NinjaTrader 8 license required: MZpack is a plugin, not standalone. You must own NinjaTrader 8, which costs $100 one-time for the platform license. Additional market data subscriptions (e.g., Kinetick) are separate and range $30–$100+/month depending on exchange complexity.
  • Forex data limitation: The mzMarketDepth indicator cannot work on forex pairs because forex brokers don't expose true Level 2 data to NinjaTrader. This is a major gap if you're a currency trader.
  • Foreign transaction fees: Pricing is displayed in EUR (€) and processed by mycommerce.com, a European payment processor. US-based buyers pay a forex conversion spread (typically 2–3%) and may incur additional foreign transaction fees from their card issuer ($2–$5 per purchase).
  • API access fees (optional): The public MZpack API is included in all tiers, but if you're building custom strategies or integrations that require significant API calls or server-side compute, you may face overage charges (not specified in public pricing—contact sales for details).
  • Broker integration costs: MZpack integrates with major NinjaTrader-compatible brokers (NinjaTrader Brokerage, Rithmic, CQG) but does not include commission rebates or special pricing—you pay standard broker rates.

Total cost to trade with MZpack full-stack: NinjaTrader 8 ($100) + market data ($30–$100/mo) + MZpack ($49/mo or €399 upfront) = roughly $180–$250/month for a beginner, or €500–$550 upfront if you buy lifetime licenses and data subscriptions annually.

MZpack Pricing vs Competitors

MZpack's main competitors in the order flow space are Bookmap and ATAS, both of which are standalone platforms (not NT8 plugins). Here's how pricing stacks up:

Platform Monthly Tier Lifetime/Annual Ecosystem
MZpack $49/mo €399 (~$435) NinjaTrader 8 plugin only; API access; automated strategies optional
Bookmap $99–$149/mo (approx.) ~$1,200/year or higher Standalone platform; cross-broker; native automation; larger user base
ATAS ~$100–$200/mo (approx.) Flexible; volume-based pricing Standalone; multi-broker support; heavy on footprint customization; Russian-developed

Verdict: MZpack is 50–66% cheaper on entry ($49 vs. $99–$150/month) and offers a lifetime option that Bookmap and ATAS don't match at the same price point. However, you're locked into NinjaTrader; Bookmap and ATAS are standalone and work with any broker. If you already use NT8 for charting or futures trading, MZpack is exceptional value. If you use TradingView, Sierra Chart, cTrader, or MetaTrader, MZpack is not an option at any price.

MZpack's API also sets it apart—Bookmap and ATAS are more closed. For algo and quant traders building custom order flow systems, MZpack's public API justifies the lower price even if Bookmap's UI is more polished.

Is MZpack Worth the Price?

For Discretionary Order Flow Traders ($49/mo): MZpack is worth every dollar if you trade futures or stocks intraday and understand footprint, cumulative delta, and DOM imbalance. The six modules are genuinely deep—iceberg detection, liquidity migration tracking, and low/high volume node clustering aren't padding; they're core professional-grade tools. Compared to Bookmap's $1,200/year, you're saving $312/year on software alone and keeping your existing NT8 infrastructure. Break-even is around 9–10 months of commitment.

For Automated Strategy Builders (€399–€599): If you want to systematize order flow without leaving NinjaTrader, the Strategies tier or Bundle is a one-time €399–€599 investment that eliminates the licensing ceiling. Backtesting and paper trading are included, so you can test strategies risk-free. For traders who value automation over discretion, this tier pays for itself in reduced commissions and fewer emotional errors within 3–6 months of live trading.

For Beginners or Casual Traders (Free Trial → Free Tier): Start with the 14-day free trial. If you're new to order flow, the learning curve is real—expect to spend weeks in paper trading before you're confident. The free tier afterward gives you indefinite access at $0 cost, which is enough to practice and build intuition without pressure to justify a monthly subscription.

For NinjaTrader 7 Legacy Users: If you're still on NT7, MZpack is one of the few order flow indicator suites with active development for that platform. The $49/mo or €399 lifetime license is cheaper than migrating your entire system to Bookmap or ATAS, making it a pragmatic choice for maintaining your setup.

For Forex Traders: Reconsider. The mzMarketDepth indicator—critical for true order flow analysis—cannot work on forex. You'd be paying for indicators you cannot fully use. Bookmap or ATAS may be better for currency traders, despite higher cost.

How to Save on MZpack

  • Lifetime License: The €399 lifetime tier is the single biggest saving. If you plan to trade for 2+ years, buy it. If you're unsure, start monthly ($49) and upgrade within the first 9 months—the monthly fees you've paid do not credit toward lifetime, but you'll have real-world validation that MZpack fits your strategy.
  • Bundle Discount: The Indicators + Strategies Bundle at €599 is €200 cheaper than buying both modules separately (€399 + €399 = €798). If you want both, bundle and save immediately.
  • No Recurring Discount Code or Coupon: As of May 2026, MZpack does not advertise seasonal discounts, student plans, or coupon codes publicly. Contact their sales team at mzpack.pro to ask about volume discounts if you're buying for a trading group or hedge fund.
  • Pay in Batch During EUR Weakness: Since pricing is in EUR and processed by a European payment processor, US-based traders save 2–3% on forex spread if they buy when the USD is strong (EUR/USD below 1.08). This is a micro-optimization, but €399 saves $10–$20 if you time it right.
  • Use the Free Trial Fully: The 14-day trial is unlimited. Spend time backtesting multiple strategies, paper trading live market hours, and joining their Discord or educational resources. You might find that the free tier afterward covers 80% of your needs, saving you the monthly subscription entirely.

Bottom Line: There are no secret discount codes or ways to materially reduce MZpack's price. The platform is already priced aggressively below competitors. Your saving strategy should focus on the tier choice (monthly vs. lifetime) and buying the bundle if you need automation, not hunting for coupons.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
